description: A $effect reads state and writes back into its own dependency, creating an unbounded update loop.
body: Failure mode: a $effect is used to compute a value by reading some state and assigning to other state that the same effect depends on, so each run schedules another, looping until the browser stalls. Detection signals: the effect re-runs continuously; CPU spikes and the tab freezes; a value oscillates; the effect both reads and writes overlapping state. Do not use $effect to derive values from other state. Safer fallback: declare the computed value with $derived so it recomputes lazily and cannot self-trigger, and reserve $effect for genuine side effects that do not feed their own dependencies.
